palette_hint: Deep parchment background #F5F0E8, near-black ink text #1A1612, with accent gold #B8963E and muted sage #7A8C6E for decorative rules and pull-quote borders.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at light/regular/bold weights with wide letter-spacing for display sizes; body in system-ui at font-weight 300 for an airy, editorial feel; pull quotes in italic Georgia at font-weight 400.
description: Maison Corvidae is a 28-seat neo-classical French supper club in a restored 1920s Portland townhouse, celebrated for its seven-course tasting menu and hand-selected Burgundy cellar.
constraints: All pages share a centered content column with max-width 780px and horizontal padding of 48px on desktop; the full-bleed page background is always #F5F0E8.
A horizontal rule in gold (#B8963E) at 1px height and 60px width, centered, separates every major content section; no other decorative images or icons are used.
The modular type scale is strictly: display 64px / section-heading 36px / sub-heading 22px / body 17px / caption/label 13px — no other sizes permitted.
Drop caps via ::first-letter appear on the opening paragraph of the Philosophy and Menu pages: 4 lines tall, gold color #B8963E, Georgia serif, float left with 8px right margin.

Pull quotes are set in italic Georgia at 24px, font-weight 400, bordered left with a 3px solid #7A8C6E bar, 24px left padding, and 40px vertical margin; they must appear at least once on the Philosophy and Cellar pages.

page_specs.philosophy: Opens with the restaurant name 'Maison Corvidae' in 64px display type centered at the top, followed by the tagline 'Where the crow dines, ceremony begins.' in 13px spaced capitals. The body tells the story of founder Céleste Morin's childhood in Lyon, her apprenticeship under two Michelin-starred chefs, and the guiding principle that every dish must honor a single seasonal ingredient above all others; the opening paragraph uses a drop cap 'C' in gold, and a pull quote — 'We do not cook to impress. We cook to remember.' — appears mid-page with the sage left-border treatment.
page_specs.menu: Presents the current seven-course tasting menu under the heading 'Le Menu du Soir' in 36px Georgia; each course is labeled in 13px spaced gold capitals (e.g. 'PREMIÈRE ASSIETTE') followed by a 22px dish name and a 17px two-sentence description of ingredients and technique. The opening paragraph describing the season's inspiration uses a drop cap 'A', and courses are separated by the centered 60px gold rule. A note at the bottom in 13px italic states that the menu changes with each new moon and that dietary accommodations require 72 hours advance notice.
page_specs.cellar: Introduces the wine program under the heading 'La Cave' with body text describing sommelier Théodore Vance's philosophy of pairing old-world restraint with Pacific Northwest terroir. Wines are grouped under three sub-headings — Bourgogne Rouge, Bourgogne Blanc, and Sélection du Sommelier — each listing four bottles with producer, appellation, vintage, and a one-line tasting note in 13px italic. A pull quote from Théodore — 'A great bottle does not complete the meal; it reveals it.' — appears between the first and second groupings with the sage left-border treatment.
page_specs.reservations: Displays reservation policy in a clean editorial layout: the section heading 'Réservations' at 36px, followed by body paragraphs explaining that Maison Corvidae seats one turn per evening at 7:30 PM Wednesday through Sunday, that parties are limited to six guests, and that a per-person deposit of $75 is collected at booking and applied to the final bill. Contact details — a dedicated phone line (503) 214-0987 and the email reservations@maisoncorv.com — are presented in 22px Georgia, and a 13px-caption note clarifies that walk-ins are never accommodated.
page_specs.find-us: Provides location and arrival information for the townhouse at 418 NW Kearney Street, Portland, OR 97209, under the heading 'Nous Trouver' in 36px Georgia. Body text describes the unmarked brass door, the interior vestibule, and the instruction to ring once and wait; a sub-heading 'Parking & Transit' in 22px precedes a short paragraph on the nearby Pearl District garage on NW 13th and the closest TriMet stop. Hours of operation are listed in a simple two-column text layout using 17px body and 13px gold-capital day labels, and the section closes with a 13px italic note that the entrance intentionally bears no exterior signage.
